channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Paraguay reopens its embassy in Jerusalem, handing Israel a rare diplomatic victory
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The small South American nation of Paraguay reopened its embassy in Jerusalem on Thursday, becoming the latest of just a small handful of countries to recognize the bitterly contested city as Israel’s capital.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bolivia extradites former anti-narcotics chief to the US to face drug trafficking charges
item.description: LA PAZ, Bolivia (AP) — Bolivia's former anti-narcotics chief was extradited to the United States on Thursday to face federal drug trafficking charges in a New York court.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Concern over Syria's chemical weapons stockpile triggers emergency meeting of global monitor
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The global chemical weapons watchdog opened an emergency meeting on Thursday to discuss the situation in Syria over concerns about the country’s stockpile of toxic chemicals in the wake of the overthrow of President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trudeau: Harris loss a setback for women in politics
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau on Tuesday addressed Vice President Harris’s loss in the 2024 presidential election, calling it a setback for gender representation in politics, during a gala promoting women in politics.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Rescue group saves 11-year-old girl floating alone in the Mediterranean for days after shipwreck
item.description: MILAN (AP) — An 11-year-old girl from Sierra Leone was found floating in the Mediterranean Sea off Italy’s southernmost island of Lampedusa, believed to be the only survivor of a shipwrecked migrant boat that had departed from the port of Sfax in Tunisia, a humanitarian group said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and MARIA GRAZIA MURRU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump invites China's Xi to his inauguration even as he threatens massive tariffs on Beijing
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has invited Chinese President Xi Jinping to attend his inauguration next month.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Florida Republican brings 'America First' bent to powerful Foreign Affairs Committee
item.description: Rep. Brian Mast’s (R-Fla.) surprising selection to be the next chair of the House Foreign Affairs Committee is the latest sign of the Republican Party being reshaped by the next generation of "America First," pro-Trump leaders.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, The Hill

item.title: Hong Kong court convicts 7 men, including ex-lawmaker, of rioting during 2019 protests
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A Hong Kong judge on Thursday convicted seven people, including a pro-democracy former lawmaker, of rioting during mob violence at a subway station at the height of the city’s anti-government protests in July 2019.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: American who says he crossed into Syria on foot is free after 7 months in detention
item.description: An American who turned up in Syria says he was detained after crossing into the country on foot seven months ago on a Christian pilgrimage.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: As tourists discover Finland's Santa Claus Village, some locals call for rules to control the masses
item.description: ROVANIEMI, Finland (AP) — Shuffling across icy ground on a cold December afternoon, lots of tourist groups poured into Santa Claus Village, a winter-themed amusement park perched on the edge of the Arctic Circle.
item.dc:creator: JAMES BROOKS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump picks businessman Leandro Rizzuto Jr. to be ambassador to the Organization of American States
item.description: President-elect Trump announced on Wednesday that he is tapping Leandro Rizzuto Jr., former Conair executive and consul to Bermuda, to be an ambassador for the Organization of American States.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: FIFA confirms Saudi Arabia as 2034 World Cup host despite human rights concerns
item.description: ZURICH (AP) — Saudi Arabia was officially confirmed Wednesday by FIFA as host of the 2034 World Cup in men’s soccer, giving the oil-rich kingdom its biggest prize yet for massive spending on global sports driven by Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strikes on Gaza kill at least 33 including children, Palestinian medics say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes pounded the Gaza Strip overnight and into Wednesday, with one attack ripping through a home where displaced people were sheltering in the isolated north. The strikes killed at least 33 people including children, Palestinian health officials said.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil’s President Lula to undergo a new surgery after initial procedure for brain bleed
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva will undergo another surgery, doctors at the Sirio-Libanes hospital where the leftist leader is being treated said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump organization officially launches new Saudi Arabia tower
item.description: Eric Trump, executive vice president of the Trump Organization, officially announced the company’s first project in Saudi Arabia on Wednesday, which consists of a 47 floor building.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Syrians flock to morgues looking for loved ones who perished in Assad's prisons
item.description: DAMASCUS (AP) — Mohammad Chaeeb spoke softly into his phone, telling a relative the grim news: He found his brother at the morgue. “I saw him and said my goodbyes,” he said. His gaze lingered on the blackened body of Sami Chaeeb, whose teeth were bared and whose eye sockets were empty.
item.dc:creator: HUSSEIN MALLA and OMAR ALBAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hundreds of tractors block central London streets as farmers protest tax change
item.description: An inheritance tax of up to 20 percent will be applied to British farms in 2026. Hundreds of farmers protested the new policy by blocking the streets of London with tractors Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea condemns South Korea as 'fascist dictatorship' after martial law scare
item.description: North Korean dictator Kim Jong Un denounced South Korea as a 'fascist dictatorship' following President Yoon Suk Yeol's failed attempt to impose martial law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea's former defense minister attempted suicide after he was arrested over martial law probe
item.description: South Korea’s former defense minister, Kim Yong Hyun, attempted suicide while in detention over the president's martial law declaration, but officials were able to stop him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Navy defeats Houthi attacks in Gulf of Aden for second time in weeks
item.description: U.S. Navy destroyers USS Stockdale (DDG 106) and USS O’Kane (DDG 77) successfully defeated a range of Houthi-launched weapons while transiting the Gulf of Aden for the second time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel bombs hundreds of sites across Syria as army pushes into border zone
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours, targeting “most of the strategic weapons stockpiles” in the country.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu wants to establish ‘relations’ with Syria but says Israel will attack it if becomes a threat
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said he wants to establish a relationship with the incoming Syrian government but warned Israel will protect itself amid strikes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syria's unaccounted for chemical weapons 'extremely worrying,' UN official says
item.description: Syrian dictator Bashar al-Assad was finally toppled after 13 years of civil war, having ruled Syria since 2000. The world's attention turns to Assad's unaccounted stockpile of chemical weapons.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here's how the US is countering the Islamic State group during Syria's upheaval
item.description: The U.S. has had troops in Syria for the last decade to battle IS. The tumult following a rebel offensive that toppled Assad has raised fears of an Islamic State resurgence.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US hostage envoy in Beirut to seek information on missing journalist Austin Tice
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. government's top hostage negotiator is in Beirut in hopes of collecting information on the whereabouts of Austin Tice, an American journalist missing in Syria for 12 years, the State Department said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour ends by shattering own record, grossing an estimated $2.2B, Pollstar says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour brought in $2.2 billion in its nearly two-year run, making it the highest-grossing tour of all time for a second year in a row, Pollstar estimates from data collected across 149 shows.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel deploys paratroopers to Syria in 'defense activities' after fall of Assad
item.description: Israeli forces released footage of their deployment into Syria following the fall of Bashar Assad's regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syrian community in Chicago celebrates end of Assad regime
item.description: It was a sea of green flags on Michigan Avenue Sunday as hundreds of Chicagoans celebrated the end of the regime in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Emmy Victor, NewsNation

item.title: Fall of Syria's Bashar Assad is strategic blow to Iran and Russia, experts say
item.description: Analysts and commentators say the collapse of Syria's Bashar al-Assad's regime is likely to reshape the power dynamics in the Middle East while signaling a critical shift in influence among world powers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New reports claim UNRWA works with terrorists, teaches hate as agency hits back at critics
item.description: UNRWA is facing new allegations that its former and present leadership have met with Hamas and other terror-related groups. This comes as another NGO says agency-run schools continue to teach hate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says Russia abandoned Syria's Assad, never should have been involved
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said on Sunday that Russia's abandonment of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad led to his downfall.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: US will remain in eastern Syria and seek to prevent IS resurgence, Pentagon says
item.description: Hours after Syrian rebels announced they had toppled Bashar al-Assad's government, the U.S. deputy assistant secretary of defense for the Middle East said the U.S. will remain in eastern Syria to prevent an ISIS resurgence.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump back on the world stage at Notre Dame reopening in Paris
item.description: Zelenskyy said his first face-to-face encounter with Trump since the election was “good and productive,” adding they had agreed to continue working together.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is welcomed by Macron to Paris with presidential pomp and joined by Zelenskyy for their talks
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron welcomed Donald Trump to Paris on Saturday with a full dose of presidential pomp as the two held a hastily arranged meeting with Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelenskyy before celebrating the grand reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: The famed Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris reopens to great fanfare
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Notre Dame is reopening its doors for the first time since a fire in 2019 nearly destroyed Paris' beloved 12th-century cathedral.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Notre Dame Cathedral reopens its doors for first time since devastating 2019 fire
item.description: France's iconic Notre Dame Cathedral is formally reopening its doors on Saturday for the first time since a devastating fire nearly destroyed the 861-year-old landmark in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Adamson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: United Kingdom cracks down on junk food TV advertisements
item.description: The United Kingdom's Department of Health estimates that 7.2 billion calories will be removed from the diets of British children.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation
